data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: Human LC-MS/MS for MBTPS1/S1P protein
project.projectDescription: To explore the molecular mechanism underlying the inflammatory symptoms, we set out to screen S1P interactors using Co-IP  and liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) and identified 29 potential candidates
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: To explore the molecular mechanism underlying the inflammatory symptoms, we set out to screen S1P interactors using Co-IP  and liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) and identified 29 potential candidates
project.dataProcessingProtocol: identified 29 potential candidates. Among them, ETFA and ETFB were identified by six and fifteen unique peptides, respectively. To this end, we performed an in-depth bioinformatics analysis of potential S1P-binding proteins using HIPPIE software (Schaefer, Fontaine, Vinayagam, Porras, Wanker and Andrade-Navarro, 2012). The protein-protein interaction network suggested that ETF might play a central role in the S1P-associated mitochondrial signaling

references.referenceLine: Chen F, Ni C, Wang X, Cheng R, Pan C, Wang Y, Liang J, Zhang J, Cheng J, Chin YE, Zhou Y, Wang Z, Guo Y, Chen S, Htun S, Mathes EF, de Alba Campomanes AG, Slavotinek AM, Zhang S, Li M, Yao Z. S1P defects cause a new entity of cataract, alopecia, oral mucosal disorder, and psoriasis-like syndrome. EMBO Mol Med. 2022:e14904
